qas.q: What type of guidance does the AFOSH program provide AF personnel?
qas.a: Safety, Fire Protection and Health Precautions.

qas.q: What is the intent of the AF HAZCOM program, as established by AFI 90-821?
qas.a: To reduce the incidence of chemically-induced occupational illnesses and injuries employees of the hazards associated with the use or handing of HAZMAT and properpreventive measures.

qas.q: Adresses general safety information associated with a vehicle maintenance shop?
qas.a: AFMAN 91-203, Chapter 32.

qas.q: Provide guidance in eliminating or minimizing hazards associated with metal works?
qas.a: AFMAN 91-203, Chapter 27

qas.q: Contains safety requirement for most common powered material-processing equipment?
qas.a: AFMAN 91-203, Chapter 18

qas.q: Covers the application of combustible powders by spray guns?
qas.a: AFMAN 91-203, Chapter 28

qas.q: Covers the use of such equipment like face and body protection?
qas.a: AFMAN 91-203, Chapter 13

qas.q: Addresses the use of drench hoses that may be used in battery shops?
qas.a: AFMAN 91-203, Chapter 19

qas.q: Agencies consulted for task requiring PPE that is not covered elsewhere?
qas.a: Management, Ground Safety, and the Base BES

qas.q: Regulates personnel performing painting duties in the paint shop?
Which chapter in AFMAN 91-203 covers safety precaution when handling substances that vaporize or burn in the presence of oxygen?
qas.a: Chapter 18

qas.q: Which chapter in AFMAN 91-203 applies to the storage of fuels in portable containers up to 660 gallons?
qas.a: Asbestos

qas.q: Name eight hazardous associated with the use of flammable and/or combustible liquids?
qas.a: 1.Explosion 2.Burns from fire 3. Chemcial Burn 4.Asphyxiation 5.Inhalation of vapors 6.Absorption through the skin 7.Skin irriation and 8. eye damage from direct contact or exposure

qas.q: What is the major cause of back injuries?
qas.a: The failure to use proper lifing techinques or mechanical lifting devices

qas.q: What are difficulties can arise from exposure to noise?
qas.a: Hearing loss, interference with communication, loss of sleep and stress reactions with possible long-term health consequences.

qas.q: What can happen if you wear contact lenses when perfoming vehicle repairs?
qas.a: They may trap toxic or dangerous substances that harmful to the eye

qas.q: What is the purpose of an AF Form 55?
qas.a: To document general and specialized job safety, fire prevention and health instruction received

qas.q: Who may service vehicle air conditioning systems?
qas.a: Certified Technicians

qas.q: When may a customer be allowed on the shop floor?
qas.a: Only when accompanied by maintenance personnel

qas.q: When must you always use a spotter when backing a vehicle?
qas.a: It your vision is obstructed

qas.q: Which psi amount is air pressure regulated when used for approved cleaning operations?
qas.a: Less then 30 psi

qas.q: Name some safety items that need to be in a mobile maintenance vehicle?
qas.a: Baking soda, fire extingushier, a container of water, first aid kit, and eye/face protection

qas.q: What is the two-man concept?
qas.a: It means to never work alone and allows each mechanic to assist and act as each other's safety observer

qas.q: What is meant by the words from cardle to grave concerning hazardous waste?
qas.a: Beginning with waste generation and ending with final disposal

qas.q: What is the objective of the Hazardous Waste Management Program?
qas.a: To ensure that hazardous waste is handled in manner that protect human health and the environment.

qas.q: Give example of solid waste?
qas.a: Trash, rubbish, garbage, liquids, or sludge that are normally discarded and are(or could be) taken to a sanitary landfill.

qas.q: What can we do to reduce the solid waste strain on landfills?
qas.a: Reduce consumption and recycle.

qas.q: What category do the majority of our automotive hazardous waste fall into?
qas.a: "F" category, nonspecific sources, such as degreasers.

qas.q: How must you dispose of empty containers that contained HAZMAT?
qas.a: Turn them in to the hazardous accumulation point or satellite accumulation point for disposal.

qas.q: What are SDSs?
qas.a: They are the primary link in the communication process between the chemical manufacturer and you, containing information that helps you handle and use products safely and accompany materials upon delivery.

qas.q: List four situation where you would refer to an SDS.
qas.a: 1. Before you handle the material for the time 2.It you have question about material for the first time 3.If you have question about how and why to store material.

qas.q: What should you know about a substance to help in choosing the right fire extingisher?
qas.a: It specific gravity.

qas.q: Who is condiered a hazardous waste generator?
qas.a: A facility owner, operator, or person who first creates a hazardous waste.

qas.q: What is one of the keys to avoiding liability?
qas.a: A good and accurate record

qas.q: State two reason why you should not mix different hazardous wastes?
qas.a: Mixing can cause violent and dangerous chemical reactions and will be very expensive to dispose of.

qas.q: How should hazardous waste containers be placed on pallets?
qas.a: In manner to reduce corrosion caused by a contact with the ground.

qas.q: What is a satellites accumulation point?
qas.a: A designed place that may store up to 55 gallons of hazardous waste or one quart of acutely harardous waste at or near the point generation.

qas.q: What can the penalties be for violating environmental laws?
qas.a: Prison terms of six months to 15 years and fine anywhere from $10,000 to $ 150,000, with cumulative fines as high as $ 50,000 per day of violation.

qas.q: What is waste minimisation?
qas.a: Any source reduction or recycling which result in the reduction of total volume, or quanity, of hazardous waste.

qas.q: Name three reasons why we should minimize the generation of hazardous waste
qas.a: Its the law, its the AF policy, and it reduce liaability

qas.q: What are ways to help minimize waste generation?
qas.a: Shop operating procdures should address waste minimization

qas.q: Sulfur Dioxide impacts
qas.a: Air pollution

qas.q: Antifreeze impacts
qas.a: Water pollution

qas.q: Used oil impacts
qas.a: Water pollution

qas.q: Carbon monoxcide impacts
qas.a: Air pollution

qas.q: Cleaning products impacts
qas.a: water pollution

qas.q: Vehicle exhaust
qas.a: Air pollution

qas.q: Fertilizer usage
qas.a: Water pollution

qas.q: What is used to help us reduce water pollution?
qas.a: Minimizing the use of particles, Report illegal dumping

qas.q: What is the best way to reduce our energy dependency and air pollution?
qas.a: Turn off lights and appliances when not using, Ensure your home has adequate insulution

qas.q: What does the commander's staff provide
qas.a: Directions, Personal Property, Deployment planning, Cargo movement, Supplies, and Equipment

qas.q: Who assist the commander in overseeing the daily operations of the squadron?
qas.a: OPS Officer

qas.q: Which section reviews and maintains installation deployment and contingency plans?
qas.a: Squadron Readiness Section

qas.q: Name the logistics manager's responsibilities
qas.a: Oversight of squadron programs and processes, system management, Resource management, squadron analysis, and strategic planning.

qas.q: Which section provides oversight of squadron compliance, training, resources, accountability, and analysis
qas.a: The operating compliance

qas.q: Which section has the key duty of unit resource advisor?
qas.a: resource management

qas.q: Which section maintains the APDPE account records for the squadron?
qas.a: Functional systems management

qas.q: Who administers the unit awards program
qas.a: CCF

qas.q: Which activity has the key duty of determining official travel entitlements?
qas.a: Passenger movement element

qas.q: Which section falls under the vehicle operation section?
qas.a: Dispatch operation, dispatch support, training/validation, and operator records and licensing

qas.q: What are the responsibilities of the dispatch operation section?
qas.a: Developing dispatch support personnel work schedules, personnel are property trained, and maintain installation

qas.q: Which element fall under the personal property section?
qas.a: Inbound, outbound, non-temporary and QC

qas.q: What action does the LGRM perform?
qas.a: Stocking, storing, issuing, managing, decentralized inventorying and inspecting DOD supplies and equipment.

qas.q: Which element manages the storage and tracking of HAZMAT?
qas.a: HTA

qas.q: Which three section make up the LGRM?
qas.a: Asset management, maintenance support and the customer support section

qas.q: Which element supports flight line activities solely?
qas.a: FSC

qas.q: What is LGRV's overall responsibility?
qas.a: To manage and maintain the installation's (wing) vehicle fleet

qas.q: What are some section that fall under the LGRV?
qas.a: Administration, MC, FM&A, CSC, and vehicle specific maintenance and repair section

qas.q: What is the responsibility of the LGRF?
qas.a: Ensure quality, petroleum products, cytogenetic fluids, and missile propellants are acquired or produced and issued safely and efficiently organization.

qas.q: Which section performs internal and external evaluations of the LGRF?
qas.a: Compliance and environmental

qas.q: Where can you find the specific duties and responsibilities of the vehicle management?
On what does the total number of work centers depend?
qas.a: Facilities, staffing, types of vehicles, and mission requirements.

qas.q: State the primary responsibility of vehicle management?
qas.a: Maintain all vehicle displaying a valid USAF or AFEMS registration number and miscellaneous, non registration vehicles and equipment with new replacement cost of over $10,000 that do not directly support a weapon system

qas.q: What must organizations do before purchasing non-registered equipment?
qas.a: Coordinate with the VFM or VMS to make parts, facilities, manpower, funds, etc. are available to support the equipment.

qas.q: What will vehicle management complete if they are responsible for a no registered asset?
qas.a: Load master records into FMIS; assess vehicle equivalent according to the information in the FMIS; and schedule all required PM&I, to include lubrication, oil changes, and other special instructions.

qas.q: What will vehicle management complete if they responsible for a non-registered asset
qas.a: Missing Answer

qas.a: Overall direction for base logistics processes related to vehicles, cargo movement, passenger movement, personal property, supplies, equipment, deployment planning and operation, fuels, and when appropriate, logistics plans.

qas.q: Who assist the commander in overseeing the daily operation of the squadron?
qas.a: OPS Officer

qas.q: Which section reviews and maintain installation deployment and contingency plans?

qas.a: Provide oversight of the squadron programs and processes, resource management, squadron analysis, system managment, stratiegic planning, metrics, and squadron trainning.

qas.q: Which section provides oversight of sqaudron compliance, trainning, resources, accountability, and analysis?
qas.a: The operation compliance; the logistics manager is the director

qas.a: Resource management

qas.a: Functional systems management

qas.a: CCF

qas.q: Which activity has the key duty of determining official travel entitlement ?
qas.a: Passenger movement element

qas.a: Dispatch operation, dispatch support, trainning/validation, and operator records and licensing

qas.q: What are the responibilites of the dispatch operation section?
qas.a: Effective use of military taxi, UDI, pick up and delivery ,and dispatch support vehicles

qas.q: Which element fall under the personal property section?
qas.a: Inbound, outbound, nontemporary, and QC

qas.q: What actions does the LGRM perform?
qas.a: Stocking, storing issuing, managing, decentralized inventorying and inspecting DOD supplies and equipment

qas.q: Which element manages the storage and tracking of HAZMAT?
qas.a: HTA

qas.a: Asset management, maintenanace support, and the customer support section

qas.a: FSC

qas.a: To manage and maintain the installation's (wing) vehicle fleet

qas.a: Admininstration, MC, FM&A, CSC, and vehicle specific maintenance and repair sections

qas.q: What is the responsibilty of the LGRF?
qas.a: Ensure quality petroleum products, cyrogenics fluids, and missle propellants are acquired or produced and issued safely and efficiently to using organization

qas.a: Compliance and environmental

qas.q: Where can you find the specific duties and responsibilites of vehicle management?
On what does the total number of work centers depend?
qas.a: Facilities, staffing, types of vehicle, and mission requirements

qas.q: State the primary responsibility of vehicle management?
qas.a: maintain all vehicles displaying a valid USAF or AFEMS registration number and miscellaneous

qas.q: What must organization do before purchasing nonregistered equipment?
qas.a: Coordinate with the VFM or VMS to make parts, facilities, manpower, funds, etc. are available to support the equipment

qas.q: What will vehicle managment complete if they are responsibile for nonregistered asset?
qas.a: Load master records into FMIS; assess vehicle equivlant according to the imformation in the FMIS; snd schedule all required PM&I

qas.q: Who is responsible for maintenance on major specialized systems mounted on standards vehicle chassis?
qas.a: The using organization

qas.q: When will the VFM/VMS not accept maintenance responsibilities for equipment?
qas.a: If the request to purchace the equipment was not coordinated with vehicle management before purchase

qas.q: Name three types of vehicles or equipment that are not authorized services in a vehicle maintenance shop?
qas.a: Those that have been disposed of as uneconomically repariable and transferred to the DLA-DS privately owned

qas.q: What is the LRS wartime mission?
qas.a: Mobilize, deploy, receive, and sustain combat forces

qas.q: What is the AF policy regarding the support for the USAF WMP?
qas.a: To have motor vehicles, manpower, tools, facilities, and shop equipment on hand to support it

qas.q: Provide the necessary forces to complete the in-theater mission?
qas.a: LRS Planner

qas.q: Provide manpower and equipment to vehicle maintainers?
qas.a: MAJCOM

qas.q: Ensure logistics requirement of all other deploying forces are met?
qas.a: LRS Planner

qas.q: Determine vehicles suited for deployment?
qas.a: Base Vehicle Management

qas.q: Deploy with sufficiently skilled personnel and equipment
qas.a: Base Vehicle Management

qas.q: Provide specific guidance to bases?
qas.a: MAJCOM

qas.q: Maintain WRM vehicles according to established guidance?
qas.a: Base Vehicle Management

qas.q: Name the team that consist of fully qualified, moblie mechanics that complete contingency repairs?
qas.a: QRMT

qas.q: Which list is predetermined with generic spare parts that deployable units must requisition mark and ship?
qas.a: TMSK

qas.q: What are the three categories of battle-damaged vehicle repairs during triage maintenance?
qas.a: Level A immediately returnableto service with minimal/ minor

qas.q: What is vehicle management's responibility concerning WRM vehicles?
qas.a: To make certain storage and maintenance of WRM vehicle are according to guidelines in prescribed instruction

qas.q: What is the one of the methods to ensure your office implements the WRM program properly and complies with all the AF directives?
qas.a: Develop a self-inspection checklist to assess your program and identify strengths and weaknesses

qas.q: What is the AF training outline called?
qas.a: CFETP

qas.q: Where can you find the list of specific responibilites for FM&A
What isn DOD policy regarding goverment vehicles?
qas.a: That goverment vehicle resource are organized and managed to ensure optimum responsiveness, efficincy and economy in support of the DOD mission

qas.q: Why did the AF adopt the VCP?
qas.a: To ensure day-to-day management of those vehicles permanently assigned to units and agencies

qas.q: Who on an installation will have a VCP?
qas.a: Each base organization with permanently assigned vehicles

qas.q: Who appoints the VCO and VCNCO?
qas.a: Unit commanders needing a VCP

qas.q: Why must vehicle be rotated?
qas.a: To help ensure they reach their programned life expectancy

qas.q: What should you consider when determining if vehicles should rotated?
qas.a: Utilization data, mission requirement, and the cost of moving

qas.q: What two parts/section must be included for documenting of the Fleet Card?
qas.a: Funding and transactions / Card administraton and management control

qas.q: What length of time must billing cycle transaction document be maintained?
qas.a: Six years

qas.q: Who is the point of contract and liaison between their unit and the LGRV on vehicle matters?
qas.a: The unit VCO and VCNCO

qas.q: When should VCOs and VCNCOs be scheduled for required trainning
qas.a: When notified by appointment letter and before departure of the current VCO and VCNCO
